meaning: adj.徒然的,无益的,虚荣的,空虚的,自负的,愚蠢的
examples: He was self-important, vain and ignorant.
I think he is shallow, vain and untrustworthy.
"People looked at it as eSpeed's vain attempt to stop their momentum, " says a veteran trader.
Some of these were contained electronically so much of the shredding was in vain.
